excerpt: IBC concludes: 'final IBC Min-Max for x360 and the siege of Falluja of 572 - 616.'
pages_or_sections: How this particular number was arrived at, para. 3
transform_note: IBC itself derived this civilian range from the local total of 800 deaths using case-specific allocation of adult-male civilians and fighters.
confidence_note: Best transparent April civilian estimate available, but not a named-victim count.

excerpt: IBC states that the viable local medical count 'ultimately reached 800.'
pages_or_sections: How this particular number was arrived at, para. 2
transform_note: Used with the IBC civilian range to derive a case-specific insurgent remainder for the April battle.
confidence_note: Mixed-status total; not used directly as a civilian or military death count.

excerpt: Among the first 600 deaths, IBC reports 160 women.
pages_or_sections: How this particular number was arrived at, para. 2
transform_note: Source-native subgroup metric retained as reported.
confidence_note: Applies only to the first 600 deaths, not the full April total.

excerpt: Among the first 600 deaths, IBC reports 141 children under age 12.
pages_or_sections: How this particular number was arrived at, para. 2
transform_note: Not converted into a u15 or u18 series.
confidence_note: Partial-period subgroup only; not directly comparable to full-period estimates.

excerpt: IBC records the competing IHM tally as 264, later 271, and finally 280.
pages_or_sections: How this particular number was arrived at, para. 2
transform_note: Retained only as conflict context; excluded from the core estimate because IBC argues the ministry later dropped Fallujah from its April statistics.
confidence_note: Important conflicting source, but not used in the core DR path.

excerpt: ArmyUP staff ride: 'I MEF reported 27 U.S. killed in action ... in the first Battle of Fallujah.'
transform_note: Used as the Coalition fatality anchor for the April episode; wider Ramadi/area casualties excluded.
confidence_note: Battle-proper figure is more suitable than wider April Anbar casualties.

excerpt: LA Times retrospective: the Marines initially estimated 'two battalions, a total of 2,500 troops.'
transform_note: Used as the lower anchor for April military person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Planning number, not a full daily strength series.

excerpt: Tony Perry reports troop strength increased 'from 2,500 to 3,750.'
transform_note: Used as the mid-battle Coalition force anchor for April military person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Contemporaneous news estimate.

excerpt: LA Times retrospective says Mattis had seven battalions by late April.
transform_note: Converted only indirectly, and conservatively, into the high military person-month scenario; not treated as seven battalions continuously inside the city.
confidence_note: High-end force-presence anchor, not a direct troop census.

excerpt: Independent extract in IBC archive: Marines had 'four battalions around Fallujah.'
transform_note: Supportive mid-battle deployment anchor for April military person-month modeling.
confidence_note: Secondary extracted journalism, but contemporaneous.

excerpt: Estes reports that the only Iraqi unit to stay the course was '400 troops with 17 U.S. Special Forces advisors.'
transform_note: Upper value includes the 17 U.S. advisors for force-presence context.
confidence_note: Useful because it distinguishes nominal Iraqi deployment from actual participation.

excerpt: Mattis warned that there were 'over 250,000 inhabitants in the city.'
transform_note: Used as the lower baseline city-population anchor before later April flight adjustments.
confidence_note: A command estimate, not a census.

excerpt: Independent extract archived by IBC describes Fallujah as 'a city of 300,000.'
transform_note: Used as the upper baseline city-population anchor before April outflow adjustments.
confidence_note: Widely repeated journalistic figure rather than administrative population microdata.

excerpt: LA Times infobox states: 'At least 60,000 flee the city.'
transform_note: Used to justify reducing the April civilian denominator below the pre-siege city baseline.
confidence_note: Contemporaneous displacement estimate, not a registration count.

excerpt: AP extract archived by IBC says Marines fought an estimated 1,500 guerrillas.
transform_note: Used as the main insurgent-denominator anchor for April.
confidence_note: Contemporaneous operational estimate, not a roster.

excerpt: IBC x453 publishes a 'final range of 581-670.'
pages_or_sections: How this particular number was arrived at
transform_note: IBC derives this from IRIN's 700+ body recoveries with overlap adjustment and assumptions about elderly vs non-elderly males.
confidence_note: Best transparent public civilian estimate for the November assault, but explicitly provisional and incomplete.

excerpt: ArmyUP summarizes one account claiming that fewer than 500 civilians remained.
transform_note: Converted the source phrase '<500' into a numeric comparison band of 0-499 for sensitivity discussion only; not used as the central denominator.
confidence_note: Important low-end claim, but difficult to reconcile with later body-recovery evidence and other contemporaneous population estimates.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports intelligence estimates of approximately 4,500 insurgents in the city by October 2004.
transform_note: Used as the upper insurgent-strength anchor for second-battle military person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Intelligence estimate, not a postbattle roster.

excerpt: ArmyUP states that Coalition forces involved in al-Fajr numbered close to 12,000.
transform_note: Used as the upper direct-assignment anchor for second-battle military person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Involved-force total; not all 12,000 were necessarily simultaneously inside the city.

excerpt: ArmyUP says approximately 10,000 of the 12,000 involved would enter the city at some point.
transform_note: Used as the central Coalition/Iraqi assault-force anchor in second-battle military person-month modeling.
confidence_note: Entry count over the course of the operation, not a simultaneous daily average.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports that 82 Americans lost their lives in the fighting in Fallujah.
transform_note: Used as the U.S. fatality anchor for the second battle.
confidence_note: Official military figure; date window extends to 31 December, slightly beyond the closed-city civilian episode window.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports 6 Iraqi killed by the end of the first two weeks of combat in the city.
transform_note: Used as the available documented Iraqi-partner fatality anchor; later Iraqi losses are not separately specified.
confidence_note: Likely incomplete for the full episode, but the best explicit figure located.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports Coalition forces killed 2,000 of approximately 4,500 insurgents.
transform_note: Used as the upper bound for second-battle combatant deaths in the core estimate.
confidence_note: Official/military estimate and therefore vulnerable to overstatement.

excerpt: Jeffrey White writes that of perhaps 3,000 insurgents, an estimated 1,200 were killed.
transform_note: Used as the lower bound for second-battle insurgent fatalities in the core estimate.
confidence_note: Still coalition-derived, but meaningfully lower than the 2,000-killed official summary.

excerpt: AFPS reported officials estimated 50,000 to 60,000 people remained in the city.
transform_note: Used as the high civilian-presence anchor for second-battle person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Official estimate and likely politically contested, but valuable as a contemporaneous high-side bound.

excerpt: Jeffrey White writes that 90-95 percent of the city's estimated 300,000 people had left before operations.
transform_note: Converted to 15,000-30,000 remaining by subtracting 90-95 percent from 300,000.
confidence_note: Derived but transparent; useful as a mid/low-side civilian-presence anchor.

excerpt: ABC/AFP relayed a U.S. military estimate that 10 to 20 percent of the 300,000 population remained.
transform_note: Converted 10-20 percent of 300,000 into 30,000-60,000 remaining.
confidence_note: Important because it documents military exit restrictions by sex/age in the same report.

excerpt: IPS reports an anonymous Red Cross official estimating at least 800 civilians killed so far.
transform_note: Retained as high-side contextual evidence only; not used as the sole core civilian numerator.
confidence_note: Method opaque and source anonymous, so unsuitable as the central estimate despite its importance as a warning that x453 is incomplete.

excerpt: Jeffrey White compares over 10,000 Coalition forces to perhaps 3,000 insurgents.
transform_note: Used as the lower insurgent-strength anchor for second-battle military person-month scenarios.
confidence_note: Useful lower bound, but still an estimate.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports 'more than 90 wounded' in the first Battle of Fallujah.
transform_note: Recorded as a lower-bound auxiliary injury claim only.
confidence_note: Lower-bound injury figure; not used in the core DR.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports that over 600 Americans were wounded in the fighting in Fallujah.
transform_note: Recorded as a lower-bound auxiliary injury claim only.
confidence_note: Lower-bound injury figure; not used in the core DR.

excerpt: ArmyUP reports 55 Iraqi wounded by the end of the first two weeks of combat.
transform_note: Auxiliary injury claim only.
confidence_note: Likely partial-period figure.
